macro_segments.title: Introduction and Overview of Three Methods of Kiddushin

micro_segments.title: Opening remarks and daf coverage
Three ways of marriage and their derivations in Mishnah vs. Gemara

macro_segments.title: Kiddushei Biah: Derivation from the word Uv'alah

micro_segments.title: The word uv'alah teaches acquisition through sexual act
Question of whether biah effects both kiddushin and nissuin simultaneously
Kal vachomer from yevamah: she can be acquired through biah, therefore regular marriage should also work through biah
Discussion of three marriage cases: yibbum, amah ivriyah, and regular marriage
Rejection of kal vachomer: amah ivriyah is not a marital relationship
Rav Ashi's resolution: distinction based on zikah status in yibbum
Tosafot on amah ivriyah: marital aspects through ye'ud despite Gemara's rejection

macro_segments.title: Kiddushei Shtar: Derivation through Comparison to Divorce

micro_segments.title: Question of how we know marriage can be effected through shtar document
Kal vachomer from kesef: money effects marriage but not divorce
Rejection based on kesef's unique power to redeem ma'aser sheni and hekdesh
Hekesh havaya l'yetziah: comparing marriage to divorce through scriptural connection
Concern of sanigor ya'aseh katigor: creating marriage through same means as divorce
Resolution: shtar for marriage and shtar for divorce are distinct documents
Distinction between fungible kesef and unique shtar in avoiding confusion
Rabbi Yose HaGalili's alternative derivation for prohibiting divorce through kesef

macro_segments.title: Learned One from Two: Attempting to Derive Methods from Combined Sources

micro_segments.title: Question: could we derive shtar from combination of biah and kesef
Rejection: biah and kesef have hana'ah (benefit/pleasure), shtar does not
Rejection: could we derive kesef from biah and shtar
Rejection: biah is not a normal mode of kinyan unlike kesef and shtar
Tosafot's rejected suggestion comparing biah to chazakah in land acquisition
Rejection: could we derive biah from kesef and shtar
Rejection: kesef and shtar can occur be'al korchah but biah in marital context cannot

macro_segments.title: Rav Huna's Kal Vachomer for Chuppah

micro_segments.title: Rav Huna: chuppah should effect kiddushin by kal vachomer
Rav Huna's suggestion: kesef which doesn't provide terumah rights effects marriage
Counter: kesef from mideoraisah allows woman to eat terumah when married to kohen
Counter: rabbinic gezeirah prevents woman who is only mekudeshet from eating terumah
Rejection of Rav Huna: biah which completes marriage should work better
Question: why doesn't kesef work since it can provide terumah rights
Tzad ha'shaveh: common denominator that all three methods are general kinyanim
Rejection based on hana'ah: kesef and biah have benefit, shtar does not
Rejection based on matzui l'mitztau: each method has unique disqualifying factor
Rava's first objection: Mishnah says three ways, not four
Rava's second objection: chuppah only works after kiddushin
Abaye's defense: Mishnah lists only those explicitly in Torah
Rav Huna's response: kesef which doesn't complete marriage should not work as well as chuppah
Tosafot HaRosh's question: does Rav Huna mean chuppah alone or chuppah twice

macro_segments.title: Practical Mechanics of Kiddushei Kesef: The Role of Amira

micro_segments.title: Baraita: man gives money/equivalent and says specific formulations of marriage
Baraita: if woman gives and says, she is not married; man must do both
Rav Pappa's question: what about intermediate cases of who gives and who says
Resolution: reisha davka - first clause is precise; seifa comes along for formulation
Woman's amira alongside man's netina makes it as if woman gave it
Alternative: man's netina with woman's amira is safek kiddushin

macro_segments.title: Shmuel's Rule on Amira Requirements and Yadayim

micro_segments.title: Shmuel: man must say 'you are mekudeshet,' 'you are me'urest,' or similar
Shmuel: man saying 'I am your husband' does not effect marriage
Parallel: divorce requires changing woman's status, not man's
Rav Pappa's question: does Shmuel hold yadayim she'einan mochiyot
Shmuel's example: 'aha' to distant nazir only works if nazir is present
Resolution: Shmuel teaches man must change woman's status, not his own

topical_tags.term: Ye'ud (master designating indentured servant for marriage)
Kal Vachomer (logical inference from major to minor)
Hekesh Havaya L'Yetziah (comparison of marriage to divorce)
Ma'aser Sheni (second tithe, redemption onto money)
Terumah (priestly portion, eaten by woman married to kohen)
Chuppah (wedding canopy/second stage of marriage, rabbinic)
Rava vs. Abaye (dispute on chuppah and three methods)
Status change (woman vs. man in marriage/divorce)
